findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Diffeomorphism/Action.lean:90` has `instance applySubgroupContinuousConstSMul (G : Subgroup (M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M))`; `code/TauCeti/Topology/Algebra/HomeomorphAction.lean:60` has the identical special transfer for `Subgroup (E ≃ₜ E)`. Mathlib's subgroup action is already generic (`mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Actions.lean:22`, `Subgroup.instMulAction`; `:31`, `Subgroup.smul_def`), and `ContinuousConstSMul` only asks for continuity of each scalar map (`mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/Algebra/ConstMulAction.lean:51`).
findings.fix: Prove the generic instance once, e.g. for `{G X} [Group G] [TopologicalSpace X] [MulAction G X] [ContinuousConstSMul G X] (S : Subgroup G) : ContinuousConstSMul S X`, and let both the homeomorphism and diffeomorphism subgroup cases use it.
findings.issue: `applySubgroupContinuousConstSMul` specializes a general subgroup-transfer fact to diffeomorphism groups, duplicating the same special pattern already present for homeomorphism groups.

summary: The full-group action statements are at the expected level, but the subgroup continuity instance is another special-case copy of a generic transfer lemma.
